title: AbbVie Inc. (ABBV) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of AbbVie Inc. (ABBV). Economic Prospect Score: 65/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: AbbVie Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 7% annually based on competitive dynamics and historical performance.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% WACC reflects AbbVie Inc.'s risk profile, sector volatility, and balance sheet quality.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate for long-run growth convergence toward nominal GDP.

thesisParagraphs: AbbVie Inc. (ABBV) is an S&P 500 constituent with established competitive advantages and consistent cash flow generation. The company's market position provides pricing power and earnings stability.

My DCF applies a 7% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability, with a 9.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks. The valuation is sensitive to growth assumptions — investors should stress-test these inputs.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is AbbVie Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 7% growth and 9.0% WACC, Opus values ABBV at $275.28/share vs $195.00 current price.

faq.question: Is ABBV overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Opus's DCF suggests ABBV is undervalued with a +41.2% margin of safety.

faq.question: Should I trade based on this?
faq.answer: No. This is not financial advice. DCF models are sensitive to assumptions. Use as one data point among many.

prospectVerdictDetail: AbbVie has navigated the Humira cliff better than expected — Skyrizi and Rinvoq are growing fast enough to restore growth by 2025. The aesthetics portfolio (Botox) adds diversification. But the company carries significant debt from the Allergan acquisition and faces ongoing pricing pressure across the pharma portfolio.
prospectKeyRisks: Skyrizi/Rinvoq facing future biosimilar competition of their own, creating another patent cliff in the early 2030s
Allergan acquisition debt ($50B+) constraining financial flexibility and limiting capital allocation optionality
US drug pricing reform (IRA Medicare negotiation) applying to AbbVie's key drugs, directly compressing revenue per patient
prospectKeyCatalysts: Skyrizi + Rinvoq combined sales exceeding $30B by 2027, fully replacing lost Humira revenue and re-establishing double-digit growth
Aesthetics and neuroscience portfolio providing non-pharma diversification with different growth and risk characteristics
Successful pipeline execution in oncology (teliso-V, ABBV-400) opening new therapeutic areas and reducing immunology concentration
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
